Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 : Search or list all Airtable relocation records for April 2026 using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Filter for records with special-handling flags , hazmat, oversized equipment, ESA, ADA, chain-of-custody, or cold-chain/cryogenic , by reviewing the Special Requirements field of each record. Key records the agent must identify include: Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy (Houston→Portland, Class 9 lithium-ion batteries, DOT hazmat), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics (Columbus→Seattle, Class 3B laser cutter, DOT 49 CFR hazmat placarding, $800 hazmat surcharge), Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago→Denver, lithium UPS batteries Class 9 hazmat, CISO-mandated chain-of-custody), Diego Castañeda / PivotPoint Labs (Brooklyn→Austin, Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill ~600 lbs, insured value $42,000, custom crating), Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack Energy (Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb solar rig, custom crating), Priya Suresh / Vectral Systems (Portland→Denver, ESA German Shepherd Atlas 85 lbs, "pet-friendly housing" in record), Terrence Nakamura / Vectral Systems (Austin→SF, ADA wheelchair accessible, 9,200 lbs split shipment, server rack custom crating), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago→Denver, Steinway Model B piano 480 lbs, specialty movers), and Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (Detroit→Chicago, cryogenic lab items requiring cold-chain at -20°C). Note: Dr. Anand Mehta / Canopy Health (Boston→Chicago) does not have an Airtable relocation record , his relocation is discovered through the QuickBooks estimate (EST-2026-0422) and the Linear chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a).
OE 2 : Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Diego Castañeda" or "CNC" or "schema validation" or "deployment gate" (or similar), or retrieve conversation history of channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_history. Find Lena Björkström's April 24, 2026 message reporting two data integrity problems on the Diego Castañeda / PivotPoint Labs CNC mill Airtable record (recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin) that the deployment gate would reject: (1) Weight ambiguity , the Airtable Special Requirements field lists the Tormach PCNC 440 as "~600 lbs" but the manufacturer spec is 525 lbs for the base unit, with the remaining ~75 lbs being the vibration-dampened custom crating weight; surcharges and insurance should be calculated against equipment weight (525 lbs), not combined shipping weight (600 lbs). (2) Incomplete equipment manifest , the record lists insured value $42,000 for the CNC mill, but precision calibration tools packed inside the same crate are not itemized and their replacement value is not included in the $42K figure.
OE 3 : Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Priya Suresh" or "ESA" or "pet deposit" or "Fair Housing" or "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Find Rachel Whitfield's April 21, 2026 email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) with subject referencing "Priya Suresh Denver Relocation" and "ESA Documentation Issue (URGENT)" (or similar). The email flags two issues: (1) the landlord at the RiNo apartment is charging a $500 non-refundable pet deposit for Priya's German Shepherd Atlas, which is a documented Emotional Support Animal , under the Fair Housing Act, landlords cannot charge pet deposits for ESAs; (2) MoveOps internal records use "pet-friendly housing" terminology instead of "ESA-accommodating housing," which creates compliance gaps because ESA relocations require different documentation (landlord acknowledgment of FHA obligations) than standard pet-friendly lease addendums. Rachel demands correction before Priya's move date.
OE 4 : Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with queries related to the client companies to find and cross-reference surcharges against Airtable records. Key invoices to discover: (a) INV-2026-0315 / PivotPoint Labs ($10,650) , includes $1,200 custom crating for CNC mill referencing "~600 lbs" and $420 cargo insurance rider referencing "declared value $42,000"; (b) INV-2026-0410 / Vectral Systems ($15,900) , includes a "$350 Pet Relocation Coordination Surcharge" for Priya Suresh (note: the invoice uses "Pet" terminology rather than "ESA"), $900 split-shipment surcharge and $400 ADA Housing Sourcing Premium for Terrence Nakamura; (c) INV-2026-0418 / GreenStack Energy ($9,440) , includes $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge for Mei-Lin Zhao and $950 overweight/specialty equipment surcharge for Joaquín Reyes; (d) INV-2026-0411 / Mosaic Robotics ($9,010) , includes $800 hazmat handling surcharge for Keiko Tanaka and $1,200 custom prototype crating surcharge for Zara Okoye; (e) INV-2026-0412 / NorthWind Technologies ($14,700) , includes $1,800 hazmat handling surcharge and $1,200 chain-of-custody security protocol (marked provisional, first-time service) for Raj Patel, and $2,200 specialty piano moving for Emilia Cruz; (f) EST-2026-0422 / Canopy Health ($12,170) , includes $1,200 chain-of-custody documentation surcharge and $800 specialty equipment crating for Dr. Anand Mehta. Note: this estimate is where the agent first discovers the Mehta relocation, since no Airtable relocation record exists for Dr. Mehta , the QuickBooks and Linear data (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a) are the primary sources for this relocation's chain-of-custody requirements.
OE 5 : Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "deployment gate" or "Diego" or "Priya" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar). Find existing DOT compliance audit tickets: (a) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack , Mei-Lin Zhao, Status: Done; (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic , Keiko Tanaka, Status: In Progress, Priority: Urgent , the key issue here is the missing signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate; (c) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert , to obtain retroactive placarding certificate from Swift Relocations, Status: To Do, Priority: Urgent; (d) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind , Raj Patel, Status: Done; (e) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack , Joaquín Reyes, Status: Done. Also find Dr. Anand Mehta's chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a, Status: In Progress) and the NorthWind retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, Priority: Urgent, tracking Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach among other NorthWind escalations).
OE 6 : Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DOT" or "placarding" or "hazmat" or "Swift" or "chain-of-custody" or "Victor Huang" (or similar) to find additional context on compliance issues. Key emails to discover: (a) Chloe Vance's DOT compliance audit email identifying 4 moves under DOT hazmat jurisdiction; (b) Chloe's urgent request to Greg Pallone (Swift) for the signed hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment; (c) Greg Pallone's reply confirming placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra per 49 CFR 172.504, signed certificate exists under Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and a scanned copy will be sent; (d) Victor Huang's (NorthWind CISO) security breach escalation email to Chloe (subject: "Immediate security incident response required Raj Patel server lab relocation"), in which Victor states the movers arrived and began loading Raj Patel's server lab before the IT security escort was on site (escort was scheduled for 9:00 AM), and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, which was explicitly prohibited. Victor is treating this as a formal security incident and demands a formal incident response owner, a written timeline, and full documentation by end of day. Note: the specific 7:00 AM crew arrival time is not stated in this email but is established via Slack (OE 7) and the Airtable audit note (recRajPatelAuditNote0411). (e) Greg Pallone's notification that Swift is implementing a 15% base rate increase plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge on all new MoveOps bookings.
OE 7 : Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "hazmat" or "DOT" or "relocation" or "special handling" or "deployment gate" (or similar) across channels including #operations (C006) and #engineering (C003) to find internal discussions about the April special-handling relocations, vendor assignments, compliance concerns, and deployment gate readiness. Key findings include Chloe Vance's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure (movers arrived 7:00 AM, escort at 9:00 AM, equipment loaded without waiting, overnight warehouse storage at SentinelChain South San Francisco facility), and the documentation integrity gap where the Raj Patel DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located.
OE 8 : Update the Priya Suresh Airtable relocation record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to correct the ESA terminology. The Special Requirements field currently uses "pet-friendly housing" language , update it to reflect ESA-compliant terminology (e.g., "ESA-accommodating housing" or similar FHA-compliant language). This correction is something the agent can do directly because it is a terminology fix, not a structural data dispute.
OE 9 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Diego Castañeda / PivotPoint Labs CNC mill deployment gate validation failures. Title should reference Diego Castañeda deployment gate blocking issues (or similar). Description must cover: (1) weight ambiguity , Airtable lists ~600 lbs but manufacturer spec for Tormach PCNC 440 is 525 lbs, the remaining ~75 lbs is crating; surcharge and insurance calculations may be based on wrong weight; (2) incomplete equipment manifest , insured value $42,000 covers the CNC mill only, but precision calibration tools packed in the same crate are not itemized and not included in the insured figure. Both issues would cause the deployment gate to reject the record. Priority should be set to Urgent or High.
OE 10 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Priya Suresh / Vectral Systems ESA documentation and FHA compliance deployment gate failure. Title should reference Priya Suresh ESA terminology and FHA compliance (or similar). Description must cover: (1) Airtable record uses "pet-friendly housing" instead of "ESA-accommodating housing"; (2) the landlord is charging a $500 pet deposit that violates the Fair Housing Act for ESAs; (3) the QuickBooks invoice (INV-2026-0410) uses "Pet Relocation Coordination Surcharge" rather than ESA-compliant language; (4) Rachel Whitfield (Vectral VP of People) has formally escalated this issue. Priority should be Urgent or High given the client escalation.
OE 11 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies chain-of-custody documentation gap. Title should reference Raj Patel chain-of-custody deployment gate failure (or similar). Description must cover: the movers arrived at 7:00 AM and began loading before the NorthWind IT security escort was confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage at SentinelChain's facility (explicitly prohibited by CISO requirements), and Victor Huang has escalated this as a formal security breach. The chain-of-custody log has documentation gaps that the deployment gate cannot pass. Priority should be Urgent.
OE 12 : Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) on the existing Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) with validation findings. The comment should note: (a) the signed placarding certificate is still missing from MoveOps files; (b) the subtask to obtain it from Swift (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert) is still in To Do status; (c) Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed via email that the certificate exists under Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538 and a scanned copy will be sent; (d) this remains a deployment gate blocker until the signed certificate is received and filed.
OE 13 : Add comments (via linear_create_comment) on other existing relevant Linear tickets with validation findings as applicable, such as updating the Dr. Anand Mehta chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a) with the current status of the medical equipment chain-of-custody documentation, or adding a note to the NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) connecting the Raj Patel chain-of-custody gap to the deployment gate validation failure.
OE 14 : Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com. Then send an email (via send_email) from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with a comprehensive deployment gate validation report. The email must include: (a) a summary of all April special-handling records reviewed, organized by flag type (hazmat, oversized equipment, ESA, ADA, chain-of-custody); (b) the specific deployment gate blocking issues found , Diego Castañeda weight ambiguity (525 lbs equipment vs 600 lbs shipping) and incomplete manifest (calibration tools not itemized), Priya Suresh ESA vs pet terminology mismatch and FHA compliance gap ($500 illegal pet deposit), Keiko Tanaka missing signed DOT placarding certificate, Raj Patel broken chain-of-custody protocol; (c) records that passed validation (Mei-Lin Zhao DOT docs complete, Joaquín Reyes oversize docs complete); (d) Airtable corrections made (Priya Suresh terminology fix); (e) blocking Linear tickets created and existing tickets updated; (f) a recommendation on deployment gate sign-off readiness , the gate cannot be signed off until the blocking issues are resolved.
OE 15 : Post a message in the #engineering channel (channel_id: C003) using conversations_add_message with a summary of the deployment gate validation findings. The message should give the team visibility into: (a) how many April special-handling records were reviewed; (b) the number of blocking issues found and their nature (weight/manifest discrepancy on Diego Castañeda, ESA/FHA compliance on Priya Suresh, missing placarding cert on Keiko Tanaka, chain-of-custody gap on Raj Patel); (c) Airtable corrections made; (d) blocking tickets created; (e) that the full report has been sent to Samira and the deployment gate cannot be signed off until blockers are resolved.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1 : Search or list all Airtable relocation records for April 2026 using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Filter for records with special-handling flags , hazmat, oversized equipment, ESA, ADA, chain-of-custody, or cold-chain/cryogenic , by reviewing the Special Requirements field of each record. Key records the agent must identify include: Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy (Houston→Portland, Class 9 lithium-ion batteries, DOT hazmat), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics (Columbus→Seattle, Class 3B laser cutter, DOT 49 CFR hazmat placarding, $800 hazmat surcharge), Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago→Denver, lithium UPS batteries Class 9 hazmat, CISO-mandated chain-of-custody), Diego Castañeda / PivotPoint Labs (Brooklyn→Austin, Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill ~600 lbs, insured value $42,000, custom crating), Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack Energy (Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb solar rig, custom crating), Priya Suresh / Vectral Systems (Portland→Denver, ESA German Shepherd Atlas 85 lbs, "pet-friendly housing" in record), Terrence Nakamura / Vectral Systems (Austin→SF, ADA wheelchair accessible, 9,200 lbs split shipment, server rack custom crating), Emilia Cruz / NorthWind Technologies (Chicago→Denver, Steinway Model B piano 480 lbs, specialty movers), and Marcus Webb / Canopy Health (Detroit→Chicago, cryogenic lab items requiring cold-chain at -20°C). Note: Dr. Anand Mehta / Canopy Health (Boston→Chicago) does not have an Airtable relocation record , his relocation is discovered through the QuickBooks estimate (EST-2026-0422) and the Linear chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a).
OE 2 : Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Diego Castañeda" or "CNC" or "schema validation" or "deployment gate" (or similar), or retrieve conversation history of channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_history. (2) Incomplete equipment manifest , the record lists insured value $42,000 for the CNC mill, but precision calibration tools packed inside the same crate are not itemized and their replacement value is not included in the $42K figure.
OE 3 : Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Priya Suresh" or "ESA" or "pet deposit" or "Fair Housing" or "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Find Rachel Whitfield's April 21, 2026 email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) with subject referencing "Priya Suresh Denver Relocation" and "ESA Documentation Issue (URGENT)" (or similar). The email flags two issues: (1) the landlord at the RiNo apartment is charging a $500 non-refundable pet deposit for Priya's German Shepherd Atlas, which is a documented Emotional Support Animal , under the Fair Housing Act, landlords cannot charge pet deposits for ESAs; (2) MoveOps internal records use "pet-friendly housing" terminology instead of "ESA-accommodating housing," which creates compliance gaps because ESA relocations require different documentation (landlord acknowledgment of FHA obligations) than standard pet-friendly lease addendums. Rachel demands correction before Priya's move date.
OE 4 : Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with queries related to the client companies to find and cross-reference surcharges against Airtable records. Key invoices to discover: (a) INV-2026-0315 / PivotPoint Labs ($10,650) , includes $1,200 custom crating for CNC mill referencing "~600 lbs" and $420 cargo insurance rider referencing "declared value $42,000"; (b) INV-2026-0410 / Vectral Systems ($15,900) , includes a "$350 Pet Relocation Coordination Surcharge" for Priya Suresh (note: the invoice uses "Pet" terminology rather than "ESA"), $900 split-shipment surcharge and $400 ADA Housing Sourcing Premium for Terrence Nakamura; (c) INV-2026-0418 / GreenStack Energy ($9,440) , includes $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge for Mei-Lin Zhao and $950 overweight/specialty equipment surcharge for Joaquín Reyes; (d) INV-2026-0411 / Mosaic Robotics ($9,010) , includes $800 hazmat handling surcharge for Keiko Tanaka and $1,200 custom prototype crating surcharge for Zara Okoye; (e) INV-2026-0412 / NorthWind Technologies ($14,700) , includes $1,800 hazmat handling surcharge and $1,200 chain-of-custody security protocol (marked provisional, first-time service) for Raj Patel, and $2,200 specialty piano moving for Emilia Cruz; (f) EST-2026-0422 / Canopy Health ($12,170) , includes $1,200 chain-of-custody documentation surcharge and $800 specialty equipment crating for Dr. Anand Mehta. Note: this estimate is where the agent first discovers the Mehta relocation, since no Airtable relocation record exists for Dr. Mehta , the QuickBooks and Linear data (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a) are the primary sources for this relocation's chain-of-custody requirements.
OE 5 : Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "deployment gate" or "Diego" or "Priya" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar). Find existing DOT compliance audit tickets: (a) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack , Mei-Lin Zhao, Status: Done; (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic , Keiko Tanaka, Status: In Progress, Priority: Urgent , the key issue here is the missing signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate; (c) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert , to obtain retroactive placarding certificate from Swift Relocations, Status: To Do, Priority: Urgent; (d) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind , Raj Patel, Status: Done; (e) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack , Joaquín Reyes, Status: Done. Also find Dr. Anand Mehta's chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a, Status: In Progress) and the NorthWind retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, Priority: Urgent, tracking Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach among other NorthWind escalations).
OE 6 : Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DOT" or "placarding" or "hazmat" or "Swift" or "chain-of-custody" or "Victor Huang" (or similar) to find additional context on compliance issues. Key emails to discover: (a) Chloe Vance's DOT compliance audit email identifying 4 moves under DOT hazmat jurisdiction; (b) Chloe's urgent request to Greg Pallone (Swift) for the signed hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment; (c) Greg Pallone's reply confirming placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra per 49 CFR 172.504, signed certificate exists under Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and a scanned copy will be sent; (d) Victor Huang's (NorthWind CISO) security breach escalation email to Chloe (subject: "Immediate security incident response required Raj Patel server lab relocation"), in which Victor states the movers arrived and began loading Raj Patel's server lab before the IT security escort was on site (escort was scheduled for 9:00 AM), and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, which was explicitly prohibited. Victor is treating this as a formal security incident and demands a formal incident response owner, a written timeline, and full documentation by end of day. Note: the specific 7:00 AM crew arrival time is not stated in this email but is established via Slack (OE 7) and the Airtable audit note (recRajPatelAuditNote0411). (e) Greg Pallone's notification that Swift is implementing a 15% base rate increase plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge on all new MoveOps bookings.
OE 7 : Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "hazmat" or "DOT" or "relocation" or "special handling" or "deployment gate" (or similar) across channels including #operations (C006) and #engineering (C003) to find internal discussions about the April special-handling relocations, vendor assignments, compliance concerns, and deployment gate readiness. Key findings include Chloe Vance's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure (movers arrived 7:00 AM, escort at 9:00 AM, equipment loaded without waiting, overnight warehouse storage at SentinelChain South San Francisco facility), and the documentation integrity gap where the Raj Patel DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located.
OE 8 : Update the Priya Suresh Airtable relocation record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to correct the ESA terminology. The Special Requirements field currently uses "pet-friendly housing" language , update it to reflect ESA-compliant terminology (e.g., "ESA-accommodating housing" or similar FHA-compliant language). This correction is something the agent can do directly because it is a terminology fix, not a structural data dispute.
OE 9 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Diego Castañeda / PivotPoint Labs CNC mill deployment gate validation failures. Title should reference Diego Castañeda deployment gate blocking issues (or similar). Description must cover: (1) weight ambiguity , Airtable lists ~600 lbs but manufacturer spec for Tormach PCNC 440 is 525 lbs, the remaining ~75 lbs is crating; surcharge and insurance calculations may be based on wrong weight; (2) incomplete equipment manifest , insured value $42,000 covers the CNC mill only, but precision calibration tools packed in the same crate are not itemized and not included in the insured figure. Both issues would cause the deployment gate to reject the record. Priority should be set to Urgent or High.
OE 10 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Priya Suresh / Vectral Systems ESA documentation and FHA compliance deployment gate failure. Title should reference Priya Suresh ESA terminology and FHA compliance (or similar). Description must cover: (1) Airtable record uses "pet-friendly housing" instead of "ESA-accommodating housing"; (2) the landlord is charging a $500 pet deposit that violates the Fair Housing Act for ESAs; (3) the QuickBooks invoice (INV-2026-0410) uses "Pet Relocation Coordination Surcharge" rather than ESA-compliant language; (4) Rachel Whitfield (Vectral VP of People) has formally escalated this issue. Priority should be Urgent or High given the client escalation.
OE 11 : Create a blocking Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Raj Patel / NorthWind Technologies chain-of-custody documentation gap. Title should reference Raj Patel chain-of-custody deployment gate failure (or similar). Description must cover: the movers arrived at 7:00 AM and began loading before the NorthWind IT security escort was confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage at SentinelChain's facility (explicitly prohibited by CISO requirements), and Victor Huang has escalated this as a formal security breach. The chain-of-custody log has documentation gaps that the deployment gate cannot pass. Priority should be Urgent.
OE 12 : Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) on the existing Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic) with validation findings. The comment should note: (a) the signed placarding certificate is still missing from MoveOps files; (b) the subtask to obtain it from Swift (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert) is still in To Do status; (c) Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed via email that the certificate exists under Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538 and a scanned copy will be sent; (d) this remains a deployment gate blocker until the signed certificate is received and filed.
OE 13 : Add comments (via linear_create_comment) on other existing relevant Linear tickets with validation findings as applicable, such as updating the Dr. Anand Mehta chain-of-custody ticket (linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a) with the current status of the medical equipment chain-of-custody documentation, or adding a note to the NorthWind retention ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) connecting the Raj Patel chain-of-custody gap to the deployment gate validation failure.
OE 14 : Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com. Then send an email (via send_email) from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with a comprehensive deployment gate validation report. The email must include: (a) a summary of all April special-handling records reviewed, organized by flag type (hazmat, oversized equipment, ESA, ADA, chain-of-custody); (b) the specific deployment gate blocking issues found , Diego Castañeda weight ambiguity (525 lbs equipment vs 600 lbs shipping) and incomplete manifest (calibration tools not itemized), Priya Suresh ESA vs pet terminology mismatch and FHA compliance gap ($500 illegal pet deposit), Keiko Tanaka missing signed DOT placarding certificate, Raj Patel broken chain-of-custody protocol; (c) records that passed validation (Mei-Lin Zhao DOT docs complete, Joaquín Reyes oversize docs complete); (d) Airtable corrections made (Priya Suresh terminology fix); (e) blocking Linear tickets created and existing tickets updated; (f) a recommendation on deployment gate sign-off readiness , the gate cannot be signed off until the blocking issues are resolved.
OE 15 : Post a message in the #engineering channel (channel_id: C003) using conversations_add_message with a summary of the deployment gate validation findings. The message should give the team visibility into: (a) how many April special-handling records were reviewed; (b) the number of blocking issues found and their nature (weight/manifest discrepancy on Diego Castañeda, ESA/FHA compliance on Priya Suresh, missing placarding cert on Keiko Tanaka, chain-of-custody gap on Raj Patel); (c) Airtable corrections made; (d) blocking tickets created; (e) that the full report has been sent to Samira and the deployment gate cannot be signed off until blockers are resolved.
